name: Stars of the South: Monte Caro Loop
name_original: Monte Caro - desde el Toscar, Parque Natural dels Ports
description: Hike the difficult Monte Caro trail from Toscar in Parque Natural dels Ports, covering 13.8 miles with 3990 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Alfara De Carles offers access to the rugged and mountainous terrain of the Parc Natural dels Ports in Tarragona, Catalonia. The region is characterized by dramatic landscapes, deep ravines, and extensive white and red pine forests. Prominent features include the Sierra de l'Espina, the Coscollosa, and the imposing peak of Caro, which stands at 1,447 meters. Numerous natural springs contribute to the lushness of the landscape, providing picturesque spots for hikers. Best hiking trails around Alfara De Carles The most popular hiking route is Stars of the South: Monte Caro Loop, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) difficult trail that takes 9 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain in the Monte Caro area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mont Caro Viewpoint – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Caro, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This trail offers spectacular views from the summit of Caro. Local hikers also love the Toll Blau loop from Caseta d'Alejandro, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through the scenic Toll Blau area, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Hiking around Alfara De Carles is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and deep ravines. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain treks. The routes in Alfara De Carles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Alfara De Carles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alfara de Carles?
A: Alfara de Carles offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to challenging mountain treks within the rugged landscape of the Parc Natural dels Ports.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For a pleasant, shorter walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6931748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau loop from Caseta d'Alejandro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.4 km long and takes about 1 hour 30 minutes. The area around El Toscar also features accessible walking trails like 'La pedra i l'aigua' and 'Els Ròssecs'.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails around Alfara de Carles showcase the dramatic landscapes of the Parc Natural dels Ports. You'll encounter extensive white and red pine forests, deep ravines, and numerous natural springs. Highlights include the imposing peak of Caro, the highest in Tarragona, and picturesque spots like the natural waterfall of the Ullal spring. The area is also home to a significant population of Spanish ibex.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alfara de Carles?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5317831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro Viewpoint – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Caro&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular views from the summit. There are also several Toll Blau loop trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14894321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau loop from les Tosses&lt;/a&gt;, providing varied distances and difficulties.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks to visit while hiking?
A: The summit of Monte Caro (1,447 meters) offers breathtaking panoramic views of the region. You can reach it via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5317831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Caro Viewpoint – Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) loop from Caro&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the natural springs in the Toscar area and the ruins of Carles castle. For specific peaks, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/688369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagalera Summit (1,035 m)&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de l'Aigua (1098 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools to discover?
A: Yes, the area is rich in water features. The natural waterfall of the Ullal spring is a notable highlight for excursions. You can also find beautiful natural pools like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4959065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll Blau&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabosa Pool&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4857460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas del Mas de Lluvia&lt;/a&gt;, which are perfect for cooling off after a hike. Don't miss &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4161216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Toll del Vidre Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alfara de Carles?
A: The hiking routes in Alfara de Carles are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the tranquility of the Parc Natural dels Ports, and the diverse terrain that offers challenges for all levels.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Alfara de Carles has a rich history. The area is known for its caves, some of which contain ancient rock paintings and engravings, such as the Cueva Pintada. You can also explore the route around the Toscar chapels, which allows you to visit Santa Llúcia, Sant Julià, and Santa Magdalena, as well as the ruins of Carles castle.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alfara de Carles?
A: The region offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for higher altitudes, particularly around Monte Caro.

Q: Is there accommodation available for multi-day hikes?
A: Yes, for those planning multi-day treks, the Caro refuge, located at 1,441 meters on Monte Caro, provides accommodation for up to 32 people. This makes it a convenient base for exploring the higher reaches of the Parc Natural dels Ports.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the area?
A: Alfara de Carles is integrated into broader hiking networks. Sections of the GR 7, a significant long-distance route crossing Spain, pass through the area. Additionally, the GR 171 connects to the GR 7 at the Mount Caro refuge, offering opportunities for extended treks.
tips: Mountain pass that is in the middle of the Portell group of mills, where it comes from the town of El Toscar and that goes in the direction of El Mascar, a group of houses that are located below Mont Caro.
Summit with observation deck and wonderful views of the Ebro Delta
Wonderful vantage point with great views

House located at an altitude of 1090 m above sea level, surrounded by forest, in the middle of the Ports Natural Park. The refuge, built in 1968 on the basis of an old farm, has a controlled camping area and a play area in the fields of the old crops. The Refugi de Caro is owned by the Unió Excursionista de Catalunya (UEC), and is managed by the rangers Josep and Rufo.

On the ground floor there is a large room as a dining room, the bar, the kitchen and two toilets and a shower. Pellet heating in the dining room with radiators in the rooms as well as wood-burning stoves in the dining room as well. On the first floor the rooms, with a total of 34 places distributed with five bedrooms with bunk beds, and two toilets with 3 showers. The bedrooms of 4, 6, 6, 8 and 10 places are completely independent.

In an external annex building there is the free shelter and the camping area. The management of the Refugi Caro UEC is integrated into the planned uses of the Ports Natural Park and promotes ecological activities and management measures, in addition to promoting recycling and waste management.

The refuge is located 100 m from the main road and is accessed by an internal path of the estate. On foot, you can also access the GR 7 Great Trail. The path that accesses from Tortosa and the plain of the Delta de l'Ebre, crosses the Natural Park and the National Hunting Reserve of the Ports of Tortosa and Beseit in the direction in the Tinença de Benifassà, Baix Maestrat, and the Matarranya region.

It has private parking at the edge of the refuge.

Crossroads and crossing point that connects the Vall d'Uixò to the west with the Toscar in the Levant, the Caro in Migjorn and Paüls in Tramuntana.
The GR 7 and the Estels del Sud route, which follows it throughout this stage, pass through this pass.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Monte Caro hike from Toscar?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult hike, requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness. It covers 22.2 kilometers with an impressive 1,220 meters of elevation gain and an equal amount of elevation loss. Hikers should be accustomed to mountain walking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail involves mostly accessible paths, but sure-footedness is required as some sections may be uneven or rocky. Expect strong ascents on rocky terrain and potentially exposed sections, winding through diverse landscapes including dense forests and more open, rocky stretches as you gain elevation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points, including the Vallfiguera Pass, the Cim de Caro Summit (1,441 m) which is the highest peak in the Massif dels Ports, and the Pallers Pass. The route also passes by the Refugi Caro UEC and the Coll de Carabasses.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Monte Caro from Toscar?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be hiked in various seasons, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, so an early start is recommended if hiking during this time. Winter can bring snow and ice to higher elevations, requiring specialized gear and experience.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within Parque Natural dels Ports, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Always check local regulations before you go, especially regarding specific areas or times of year.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Monte Caro trail from Toscar?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this route is from Toscar. Specific parking information for this exact trailhead isn't detailed, but typically, trailheads in natural parks have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or park information for the most precise parking locations near Toscar before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Parque Natural dels Ports?
faqs.answer: There are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Parque Natural dels Ports. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific requirements, especially for larger groups or during peak seasons.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notable sections include parts of the Estels del Sud, Del Santuari de Pinós als Ports, and the E23. It also crosses segments of El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, Pedals dels Ports, and the E24.

faqs.question: Is there a way to make this challenging hike more manageable?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the single-day ascent from Toscar is strenuous, there are multi-day guided options available. For example, some guided treks cover a similar distance over two days, including an overnight stay at the Refugi Caro UEC. This allows for a more relaxed pace and deeper immersion in the mountain environment.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, high-energy snacks, appropriate hiking boots with good ankle support, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A map, compass or GPS device, and a first-aid kit are also highly recommended. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is essential, especially on exposed sections.

faqs.question: Can I find food or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Natural dels Ports is known for natural springs, their reliability can vary by season. It's best to carry all the water you'll need for the entire hike. The Refugi Caro UEC, located near the summit, may offer food and water services, but it's advisable to check their operating hours and availability in advance.
